title: Quake Champions
notes: Overally runs pretty smooth, but only on low settings. I had to set everything to low to avoid crashes and extreme freezes. When I start a match, there are still some short freezes for a few seconds, but then everything gets smooth after I traversed the whole map (shader caching).

title: Quake Champions
notes: Can't connect to the server (Error 104)

title: Quake Champions
notes: Severe stuttering present possibly due to shaders compiling. Crashed on high settings after loading a tutorial match and firing a few rounds from machine-gun. Game actually starts and gets through splash-screens and intro movie compared to 3.7-4 Proton however. Main Menu works fine.

title: Quake Champions
notes: as of 9/03/2018 it is working. Prior to this date it would crash consistently, some times in the menu but mostly shortly after joining into a match or training. it might crash if you alt-tab out (at least for me) but so long as I dont do that I can successfully play, so far have played a few bot matches with out issue. few visual glitches in the store (textures not loading on faces). Had to run the game at lowest settings to get frame rates I could reliably play at but that might also be a symptom of my older hardware + compatibility, other wise runs very smooth in menus and does not take too long to load. will occasionally get stuck in load screens and lock up the whole pc.

title: Quake Champions
notes: as of today 9/6/2018 (after the latest 3.xGB patch), I started getting crashes at the intro, or getting error 104 on start

title: Quake Champions
notes: Error 103 in-game, D3D11 unsupported query type 6; "jack" backend initialization failure, D3D11_BIND_STREAM_OUTPUT failure, 314.885:0008:0056:fixme:winhttp:set_option unimplemented option 84
Unplayable; network problem report. Maybe some kind of anti-cheat is blocking Proton.

notes: Create a symbolic link to older version of gnutls as described in https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/225 to workaround connection issue.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Error 103
title: Quake Champions

notes: Using the libgnutls workaround. Runs fantastically overall, but eventually crashes at some point during an actual match. None of the suggested tweaks improve the situation.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Followed this guide: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/225#issuecomment-426213350 except using libgnutls.so.30.14.11 (check which is the highest one on your system by using the command "cd /usr/lib/ && ls | grep libgnutls.so" in terminal). Even with fix you need to turn details down to low or you'll crash when firing a beam weapon and even with that you might still crash in MP. Also very stuttery until the shaders are loaded.
title: Quake Champions

notes:
as mentioned needs https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/225 needs to be done

Set graphics to lowest, was crashing when loading in with graphics higher than low, drops frames
title: Quake Champions

notes: Need libgnutls worked around, then runs flawlessly.
title: Quake Champions

notes: copied libgnutls.so.30.14.11 from /usr/lib to /.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_64/ and ran ln -s libgnutls.so.30.14.11 libgnutls.so.26 to make the network connection work with this game. Works without issues. 2560x1440 all high, resolution scale 100%, FPS 60-120, mostly around 85
title: Quake Champions

notes: Error 103 can be fixed with the gnutls workaround. Since Stream Output is enabled in Proton 3.16 the game does not crash anymore and is playable.
title: Quake Champions

gpu: X.Org Radeon RX Vega (VEGA10, DRM 3.26.0, 4.18.14-arch1-1-ARCH, LLVM 8.0.0)
notes: Game starts but gives a "Network is unavailable" error afterwards it only gives me the option to close the game (Cuz multiplayer only..).
title: Quake Champions

notes: Game runs, connects fine after the tweaks mentioned by others here, but the map Lockbox crashes upon load (supposedly Sorlag's ability crashes the game as well.)
title: Quake Champions

notes: you need 396.54.09, proton 3.16 beta, and you need to  "ln -s /usr/lib/libgnutls.so /usr/lib/libgnutls.so.26" in "/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu"
title: Quake Champions

notes: The libgnutls hack is mandatory but also not bullet proof. A costant is that if I play on Windows chances it works after on Linux are 100%. If I just keep playing on Linux maye on reboot it won't work anymore. Then I can try forever and it won't work on Linux until I play on Windows. I feel like blacklisted ... or something similar, super weird. Also shaders compiled at runtime makes it somehow unplayable the first time in every map.
title: Quake Champions

notes:
Runs after doing gnutls sym link but performance is pretty poor 50-70fps compared to 180-200 fps in windows VM.

Bad stuttering on map start until everything cached.

Borked, but would be Bronze if it wasn't for the gnutls issue maybe Silver if initial cache stuttering can be solved.

Note steam system info incorrectly reports my GPU as I use DRI_PRIME=1 to run on RX580, not the lower end gpu.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Will not even start the game - tried ln -s /usr/lib/libgnutls.so /usr/lib/libgnutls.so.26 but that didn't work at all
title: Quake Champions

notes:
launch options(maybe you have a different path/version of the lib): 
ln -s ~/.steam/steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/amd64/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgnutls.so.30.14.11 ~/.steam/steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_64/libgnutls.so.26 && STEAM_RUNTIME_PREFER_HOST_LIBRARIES=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1  DXVK_HUD=0 %command% &&rm ~/.steam/steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_64/libgnutls.so.26

Fix choppy framerate:
- NO VSYNC
- Go  to ingame options and and move the max fps slider from 60 (default) to something higher like 220, but NOT to infinity. 

After that the game runs abolutely perfect with my setup. Played for 12 hours.
title: Quake Champions

notes:
ldconfig -p|grep libgnutls|cut -d ' ' -f 4|head -n 1|tr -d ' '|xargs -I {} sh -c 'ln -s "$1" ~/.steam/steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/pinned_libs_64/libgnutls.so.26' - {}



(works fine after this workaround)
title: Quake Champions

notes: Network error 103
title: Quake Champions

notes: Error 103
title: Quake Champions

notes: Works after creating symlink. Performance is not great, but the main issue is that the game is constantly stuttering/freezing during the game, which makes it basically unplayable. Disabling esync/other options from the other reports isn't helping in my case.
title: Quake Champions

notes: After creating the symlink (sudo ln -s /etc/lib/libgnutls.so /etc/lib/libgnutls.so.26) I never got the Network Error 103 anymore. Quake Champions has a lot of shaders. After playing alone on Custom Game Servers on every map, looked at every Outfit and Weapons, the Game runs without stuttering. First the mouse worked fine but after the update to kernel 4.19.1-arch1-1-ARCH (+ over 100 other updates) it was unplayable with the mouse stuttering and switching to borderless mode fixed the mouse stutters.
title: Quake Champions

notes:
Runs well (low fps when first loading in maps)
i assume its just loading the shader cache
title: Quake Champions

notes: Although cpu and gpu load are below 50%, fps didn't get higher than 90 fps in game with ultra settings, and sometimes I have some issues with sounds... I only have to symlink the gnutls lib to get rid of this famous error 103
title: Quake Champions

notes: The game has updated (November 18) with a 3.8GB patch and now works without fixes and workarounds.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Game works out of the box, performance drops a bit when loading anything (maps, enemies, guns etc.)
title: Quake Champions

notes: I have old GPU, so playing on low settings. After November patch: no crashes any more (specifically tested playing Sorlag - no crash when using ability), no connection issue any more. Excessive stutter on first map load, but it disappears pretty quickly. Decided on silver rating only because performance is considerably lower than on Windows. Finally, it's playable out of the box :).
title: Quake Champions

notes:
Works out of the box since "November Update" with proton 3.16-4 . It also works in higher settings now (except textures stuck in LOW by the game itself). Performance in higher settings is OK but frame rate is not exceptional (max 60fps in Ultra). Let's hope they provide a Vulkan option! 
Video:  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sO4IfxGgUec
title: Quake Champions

notes: With the latest patch it now works perfectly without any tweaks at all. All maps. You do need 396.54.09 to get stream output.
title: Quake Champions

gpu: ATI Technologies Inc. AMD Radeon R9 290
notes: Works with the 3.16.4 proton version, heavy stuttering at first, but clears up for the most part by the end of one or two games. Silver because of the performance, otherwise would be Platinum because it runs out of the box.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Game seemed to start fine but I got an error saying I couldn't connect to the network and I couldn't play. Apparently this is fixed for most people but it didn't work for me.

notes: Everything is working technically, but the performance penalty is definitely there. If you've got a beefy machine you should have no issues.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Runs, but there is some stuttering which can lead to deaths.
title: Quake Champions

notes:
Used command line from some other comment, maybe not all of that is necessary anymore.
It runs but that's about it. In windows on startup you sometimes see compiling shaders. From the comments I get that on linux it does this in game. If you play 2 hours a map vs bots maybe it's OK vs the bots, but that's not the point of this game. It's really unplayable online as it is
title: Quake Champions

notes: Minor stuttering after map load, usually gone before the round starts.  Otherwise perfect.
title: Quake Champions

notes: All parts of the game are working.  Each map, effect, character, and cosmetic will cause severe stutter until shader cache is generated.  After that, there is still abysmal performance and frequent stuttering, but it's the same on Windows.
title: Quake Champions

notes:
Played the game for several hours with horrible stuttering, randomly decided to disable esync and the game runs perfectly now. Running on low I get around 100 fps. Here are my launch options: 
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% --set /Config/CONFIG/maxFpsValue 144
title: Quake Champions

notes: Initially the game will run like absolute garbage, but once the cache has filled it runs great. I have to play on every single map for about 10 minutes before it becomes playable.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Can confirm, disabling Esync just makes the game run near perfect: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% --set /Config/CONFIG/maxFpsValue 144
title: Quake Champions

notes: I am not sure if disabing esync helps with anything, but new NVIDIA drivers significantly improve performance. There are no technical problems whatsoever.
title: Quake Champions

notes: After shader caching everythings runs with close to native performance.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Doesn't seem that disabling esync did anything. Shader caching takes a little while and the game stutters a lot while they are being loaded, but after that the game runs flawlessly.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Little initial stutter on shader compilations, but smooth as butter when done.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Runs without gnutls workaround in latest proton, but is very stuttery for the first minute or so of a match. Usually goes away in time for the warmup to end. Despite that, is still quite playable.

notes: In the Nvidia Control Panel, forcing the Full Composition Pipeline removed all stuttering from the game. As a result, this game gets a Gold from me.
